description: validateAndTransitionVault accepts _now: Timestamp but does not consume it. The Rust source-of-truth aggregate.rs::configure(vault, path, now) reserves the parameter for future use, so naming the TS parameter _now matches Rust intent, but a careless future Date.now leak would still pass the existing tests. Resolved in-place by adding a JSDoc explaining _now is reserved for parity with Rust and by tightening one test to assert structural equality against a frozen expected vault for varying timestamps.

evidence.snippet: /** _now is reserved for parity with Rust aggregate.rs::configure */ export function validateAndTransitionVault(vaultId, path, _now): Vault

resolution.notes: Added JSDoc and structural-equality regression guard test. A future Date.now leak would now break the test.
